tools.name: read_code
tools.description: Extracts code surgically via tree-sitter AST — not repo-wide search. Eight modes; envelope: mode, focus, summary, data, hint, related_modes, next_calls, meta (meta.tokens_returned, meta.credits, meta.charges_usage). Hosted: 5 credits per success; failures free. Cheapest path: mode file or outline wi

tools.name: find_code
tools.description: PREFER over native Grep when location is unknown. Ripgrep + AST ranking across web, mobile, CLI, and monorepos. Envelope: intent, focus, summary, data.matches, files_hit, next_calls, meta.credits (5 hosted). intent: snippet=pasted line; symbol=known name; concept=topic+also_try synonyms; everywhere=

tools.name: audit_headers
tools.description: Audit a public HTTPS URL the user deployed — security grade A–F, SSL, headers, cookies, health (ALIVE/DEGRADED/BROKEN), exposed secrets, tech stack. Read plain_summary first; only drill into security_headers or secrets if grade is poor. quick ~1–3s; scan_depth=deep for secret scan (~8–12s). 6 credit

tools.name: keep_thinking
tools.description: Structure multi-step debugging and planning across tool calls — not a one-shot think. Tracks hypotheses, observations, plans; detects loops via lastActions; riskLevel high/critical blocks dangerous edits (drop table, prod deploy). Loads projectBrief (stack, key_paths, project_memory recall) on local

tools.name: check_package
tools.description: Check a public registry package before install or version bump — frameworks (next, react, vite), payments (stripe), auth (next-auth, clerk), ORMs (prisma, drizzle). task=check: exists, slopsquat, deprecation, CVEs (pass version); task=upgrade|migrate: breaking_changes + migration_steps from release

tools.name: check_test
tools.description: Run tests and return structured health — fix_first, broken_areas, failure_clusters, coverage_by_area, blind_spots, deploy probes. Not for finding which files to edit (find_code). Workflow: task=detect (0 credits — framework, command, test file count, missing-test gaps) → task=run once (1 credit host

tools.name: Zephex_dev_info
tools.description: Expert developer playbooks — not your repo. Stripe webhooks & checkout, Supabase RLS, Next.js auth (clerk, next-auth), payment flows, CSP/HSTS, deploy patterns. operation=search finds entries by question; operation=get returns full guidance by slug from search. Read summary and checklist first. 2 cr

tools.name: project_memory
tools.description: Persists short notes about this project across chat sessions — facts you learned that are not in source code. Not a repo scanner (use get_project_context for stack). remember: saves title (max 80 chars), content (max 500 chars), type, optional area/tags — rejects API keys, tokens, and instruction-po

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_project_context" references sensitive artifacts (env var, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Scans manifests and config — one topic slice per call. Detects stack, scripts, monorepo, API routes, auth/DB providers, integrations, env vars. Secret-sanitized…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "audit_headers" references sensitive artifacts (secret, cookie). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Audit a public HTTPS URL the user deployed — security grade A–F, SSL, headers, cookies, health (ALIVE/DEGRADED/BROKEN), exposed secrets, tech stack. Read plain_…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "check_test" appears informational but accepts parameters (command) that enable code/file/secret access.

evidence.snippet: task, path, session_id, file_filter, area, question, command, with_coverage, failed_only, diff_base, include_flaky, include_missing, timeout_ms, detail_level, coverage_top, limit, inline_files

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: explain_architecture, find_code, audit_headers, check_package, check_test, Zephex_dev_info, project_memory · Private data: get_project_context, explain_architecture, read_code, find_code, audit_headers, keep_thinking, check_test, project_memory · Public sink: audit_headers, Zephex_dev_info · Destructive: get_project_context, keep_thinking, check_test, project_memory.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: explain_architecture, find_code, audit_headers, check_package, check_test, Zephex_dev_info, project_memory · Private data: get_project_context, explain_architecture, read_code, find_code, audit_headers, keep_thinking, check_test, project_memory · Public sink: audit_headers, Zephex_dev_info · Destructive: get_project_context, keep_thinking, check_test, project_memory

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
